Microsoft is among the companies bypassing Intel's proprietary x86 architecture in favor of Arm-based designs for data centers. Furthermore, Microsoft's collaboration with Arm for Windows-based laptops in 2024 has contributed to Intel losing market share in the PC sector.

He contends this concentration contradicts the principle of diversification, potentially leaving average investors vulnerable to significant losses if these tech giants underperform.

Geoffrey Hinton, known as the "Godfather of AI," has supported Elon Musk's legal efforts to block OpenAI's transition from a nonprofit to a for-profit structure. T. Rowe Price launched two actively managed ETFs: the T. Rowe Price Global Equity ETF (TGLB) and the T. Rowe Price Equity Research ETF (TIER). TGLB, managed by Peter Bates, has a 0.46% expense ratio and includes Microsoft (5%), Amazon (4.5%), and Meta (4.3%) as top holdings. TIER has a 0.38% expense ratio, excludes U.S. equities, and focuses on over 350 global stocks.

Meta Platforms is exploring the acquisition of AI voice-cloning startup PlayAI for an undisclosed amount. As of March 31, 2025, Meta held $70.23 billion in cash and equivalents. The company has previously invested in Scale AI and hired AI researchers from Google, Sesame AI, and OpenAI.

Morgan Stanley analysts suggest CoreWeave's potential acquisition of Core Scientific could increase the latter's market cap by $2.8 billion. Core Scientific has a 12-year hosting partnership with CoreWeave valued at $10 billion, providing 590 MW of capacity. CoreWeave serves AI workloads for clients including Microsoft and Meta.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk endorsed Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's view that AI must produce 'socially useful' results and create economic surplus. Nadella emphasized that AI progress should be measured by real-world outcomes in healthcare, education, and productivity rather than technical benchmarks.

Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sanchez began wedding celebrations in Venice, Italy, on June 26, 2025. Notable guests included Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Oprah Winfrey, Kim Kardashian, and Amazon CEO Andy Jassy.

Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff stated that AI handles 30% to 50% of tasks in engineering, coding, and support with 93% accuracy. The article notes that Microsoft recently announced its third round of 2025 layoffs affecting sales and customer-facing roles due to AI-related changes.

The Invesco QQQ ETF has confirmed a Golden Cross, signaling a potential long-term uptrend. The ETF closed at $539.78, trading above all key simple moving averages. Microsoft (up 17.09% YTD), Meta (up 18.85% YTD), and Netflix (up 44.25% YTD) are identified as top performers among the QQQ's top 10 holdings. The index is approaching its February 52-week high of $540.81.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman dismissed rumors of a feud with Microsoft, describing his relationship with Satya Nadella as 'really wonderfully good' despite some tensions. Altman also addressed Meta's aggressive recruitment of OpenAI talent, noting that employees remain committed to AGI. Year-to-date, Microsoft shares have risen 17.09% and Meta shares have surged 18.85%.

Economist Craig Shapiro warns that AI-driven labor displacement could affect 25% of U.S. jobs by 2030, arguing that Federal Reserve interest rate cuts are ineffective against this structural shift. Citing Goldman Sachs and Harvard studies, Shapiro suggests fiscal and regulatory solutions like universal basic income. Several AI-linked ETFs are listed with varying YTD performance, including Defiance Quantum ETF at 9.42%.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives raised his price target for Microsoft to $600 from $425, citing a 'massive adoption wave' of AI-driven Azure services. This target represents a 23% upside from Tuesday's closing price. Microsoft shares are up 17.09% YTD, and the company currently holds a consensus 'buy' rating with an average price target of $517.85 among 29 analysts.

Senator Bernie Sanders is advocating for a 32-hour workweek to mitigate the impact of AI-driven job displacement. He introduced the 'Thirty-Two Hour Workweek Act' last year. The proposal follows Microsoft's announcement of its third round of layoffs for 2025, which affected sales and customer-facing roles due to AI-driven workforce shifts.

Phoebe Gates, co-founder of the fashion-tech startup Phia, uses ChatGPT to reverse-engineer viral social media content for marketing. Phia, which raised approximately $850,000, is a browser extension that compares prices across 40,000 retail sites. Gates noted that her father, Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, did not provide funding for the venture.

Elon Musk agreed with a social media post stating that Google lost the AI race to OpenAI despite having superior resources. Following the release of ChatGPT in 2022, Microsoft integrated AI features into its offerings, pressuring Google. Meanwhile, Musk's AI venture, xAI, is reportedly planning a $300 million share sale at a $113 billion valuation.

Microsoft's open-source AI platform for quantitative investment, Qlib, has gained significant popularity on GitHub, attracting over 6,000 stars this month. The platform now features an integrated R&D Agent designed to automate the functions of a quant fund by analyzing research and finding alpha without manual intervention.

Microsoft develops and licenses consumer and enterprise software, organized into three segments: productivity and business processes, intelligence cloud, and more personal computing. Financial analysis shows Microsoft has a P/E of 37.56, P/B of 11.22, and P/S of 13.44. The company reported $40.71 billion in EBITDA and $48.15 billion in gross profit. Its revenue growth is 13.27%, and it maintains a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.19. Compared to industry peers, Microsoft shows potential undervaluation in P/E and P/B ratios, but higher P/S, lower ROE, and slower revenue growth.

Fidelity International portfolio manager George Efstathopoulos is bullish on Chinese tech stocks, citing an innovation surge and government support. Chinese companies like Alibaba and BYD are outperforming U.S. tech giants, with Alibaba up over 33% and BYD up nearly 46% year-to-date. In contrast, U.S. tech stocks like Amazon and Apple have declined. Valuation comparisons show U.S. tech giants trading at premium multiples, with Microsoft at a forward P/E of 32.4 and Nvidia at 33.6, while Chinese counterparts like Alibaba and JD.com trade at significantly lower forward P/Es of 11.6 and 7.8, respectively.

Meta Platforms held acquisition discussions with AI video startup Runway before ultimately investing $14.3 billion in Scale AI in June, securing a 49% stake. Meta also recruited Scale AI founder Alexandr Wang and other talent. The company has pursued various AI acquisition targets this year, including Safe Superintelligence and Perplexity AI. Additionally, Meta is collaborating with Anduril Industries on a joint bid for U.S. Army augmented reality contracts as part of a $22 billion modernization initiative. Meta stock closed at $698.53 on Monday, up 16.57% year-to-date.

Alibaba Cloud plans to expand its AI services, including its Qwen large language models and Model Studio, to overseas data centers by 2025. The company currently operates 29 data centers globally and intends to open a second South Korean facility by June. Alibaba is partnering with Microsoft, Siemens, and Nvidia to strengthen its AI ecosystem and has committed at least 380 billion yuan ($52 billion) to cloud and AI infrastructure over the next three years. Alibaba shares have surged over 51% in the last 12 months.

Trivariate Research founder Adam Parker is reversing his bearish outlook on U.S. corporate earnings, stating that fears regarding tariffs and inflation were overblown. Parker noted that the top 50 U.S. companies, including Microsoft, have shown resilience and remain largely immune to these pressures. While acknowledging potential short-term economic cooling, he remains optimistic about long-term growth driven by artificial intelligence. Since April 2, Microsoft stock has risen 27.17%, while Ford Motor Co. faces a net impact of $1.5 billion from tariffs this year.

The article highlights the career of FedEx founder Fred Smith, who turned a college paper concept into a $54 billion logistics giant. It draws parallels between Smith and other successful figures who faced academic skepticism or dropped out, including Bill Gates, who founded Microsoft after leaving Harvard. The piece emphasizes that while academic doubt may exist, relentless execution is key to building legendary brands.

Microsoft was surpassed by NVIDIA as the world's most valuable company, with NVIDIA's market cap exceeding $3.7 trillion. Additionally, Microsoft has delayed the commercialization of its in-house AI chip, Maia (code-named Braga), by at least six months, moving the target from 2025 to 2026 due to design changes and staffing constraints.

SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son aims to position his company as a leader in artificial super intelligence, competing with Microsoft, Amazon, and Google. Furthermore, Tesla CEO Elon Musk endorsed Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's view that AI should be socially useful, and Nadella praised Palantir Technologies' Forward Deployed Engineer model.

Bill Gates, co-founder of Microsoft, publicly urged the U.S. government to maintain funding for the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) in response to potential cuts. Gates warned against reducing global health aid, citing research that such cuts could lead to 8 million additional child deaths by 2040.

Microsoft options activity shows a bullish sentiment, with 121 calls totaling $89,012,502 versus 52 puts totaling $3,753,662. Analysts from Wedbush, Wells Fargo, Citigroup, and Morgan Stanley maintain positive ratings with price targets ranging from $530 to $605. The stock is currently trading at $498.44, with an earnings announcement expected in 29 days.

Microsoft was a key driver of the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund's (XLK) 22% rally in Q2 2025. Microsoft shares gained 31.8% during the quarter, contributing 420 basis points to the index's performance with a 14.24% weighting.

Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates warned that U.S. cuts to global health aid could lead to 8 million additional child deaths by 2040, citing a Lancet study. Gates urged Congress to restore funding for programs like PEPFAR and the Global Fund. He also criticized Elon Musk and Donald Trump for supporting USAID funding cuts, which he claims threaten food and medicine supplies.

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella stated in a fireside chat that integrating quantum computing with AI will be a game-changer for scientific fields like chemistry, physics, and material science. Meanwhile, Quantum Computing, Inc. shares fell 10% this week following a $200 million private placement announcement.

Forbes estimates that if Bill Gates had retained his original Microsoft stock instead of selling and donating shares, his net worth would be $1.2 trillion, making him the world's first trillionaire. Currently, Gates holds an estimated 0.9% stake in Microsoft valued at $28 billion, while he and his ex-wife Melinda have donated over $60 billion to their foundation.

Nvidia's market valuation reached $3.8 trillion, surpassing Microsoft to become the world's most valuable company. The broader market rally was driven by Nvidia's earnings and strong Q3 results from Micron, which reported $9.3 billion in revenue. Meanwhile, Palantir shares hit record highs, and Hims & Hers Health shares dropped 34% following the end of its partnership with Novo Nordisk.
Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates attended the $50 million wedding of Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez in Venice. During the trip, Gates visited the Gallerie dell'Accademia to view art.

Palantir shares fell over 10% on Friday, with Jim Cramer speculating the decline might be linked to a FTSE Russell index rebalance. Despite the drop, the stock remains up 93% year-to-date. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has previously praised Palantir's 'Forward Deployed Engineer' model.

Microsoft is mandating that employees use internal AI tools, with managers instructed to incorporate AI usage into performance reviews. Julia Liuson, head of the division overseeing GitHub Copilot, stated that AI usage is now core to every role. This initiative aims to boost internal adoption of Copilot amid increasing competition from services like Cursor.

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella described Palantir's Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) model as 'fantastic' at a Y Combinator event. Palantir's FDE approach involves embedding engineers directly with customers to build products at the point of need, contrasting with traditional consulting. Palantir recently raised its 2025 revenue guidance to $3.89-3.90 billion, projects adjusted free cash flow of $1.6-1.8 billion, and has seen its shares rise over 492% in the past 12 months.

OpenAI and Microsoft are in a dispute over a contractual clause that would limit Microsoft's access to Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) once achieved. Both companies stated that talks are ongoing. Reports indicate OpenAI plans to reduce the revenue share paid to Microsoft from 20% to 10% by 2030. OpenAI's annualized revenue run rate has reached $10 billion, and ChatGPT has 500 million weekly active users as of March.

Analyst Dan Ives raised his price target for Microsoft to $600, predicting the company could reach a $5 trillion market cap within 18 months. Ives cited a 'massive adoption wave' of AI-driven Azure services and expects an incremental $25 billion in AI-related revenue next year. Microsoft shares rose 0.44% to $492.27 on Wednesday.

Meta launched an AI-powered summary feature for WhatsApp that uses Private Processing technology to generate summaries of unread messages without Meta or WhatsApp accessing the content. The feature is optional, currently available in the U.S. with English support, and will expand later this year. This follows reports that the U.S. House of Representatives banned WhatsApp on staff devices due to security concerns.

A federal judge ruled in favor of Meta in a lawsuit filed by authors, determining that using copyrighted books to train AI models is protected under the fair use doctrine. Judge Vince Chhabria noted a lack of evidence regarding market dilution. The ruling follows similar legal outcomes for Anthropic, though other lawsuits against companies like OpenAI and Microsoft regarding AI training data remain ongoing.

Wall Street reached record highs at the end of June as geopolitical tensions eased. Nvidia reclaimed its position as the world's most valuable company with a market capitalization of $3.85 trillion, surpassing Microsoft. Markets are pricing in potential interest rate cuts, while trade negotiations with China have progressed, despite a halt in trade talks with Canada.

Microsoft has postponed the commercialization of its in-house AI chip, Maia, from 2025 to 2026 due to design changes and staffing constraints. The delay also reflects concerns about the chip lagging behind Nvidia's Blackwell, which launched in 2024. In 2024, Microsoft purchased approximately 485,000 Nvidia 'Hopper' chips. Nvidia recently surpassed Microsoft in market capitalization.

Alphabet Inc. appointed Kobi Bar-Nathan as the new finance chief for Google Cloud in June 2025. Bar-Nathan previously held finance leadership roles at Oracle Cloud and Microsoft Cloud. Google Cloud reported $12.3 billion in revenue for the first quarter, a 28% year-over-year increase. OpenAI has reportedly shifted its cloud services deal from Microsoft Azure to Google. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives noted Microsoft Azure's dominance in the cloud and hyperscaling sectors, projecting that as Microsoft's AI and cloud business grows from 20% to 50-60% of its total operations, it will lead to a fundamental revaluation of the company.

SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son aims to make SoftBank a leading platform provider in artificial super intelligence (ASI) within a decade, positioning the company to rival Microsoft, Amazon, and Google. SoftBank has invested $6.5 billion in Ampere and committed up to $40 billion to OpenAI, with total investment in OpenAI reaching $32 billion since 2024. SoftBank reported fourth-quarter net sales of 1.94 trillion yen ($12.69 billion).

Microsoft has moved Chief Information Security Officer Igor Tsyganskiy from its traditional security organization to the Cloud + AI group, where he now reports to Executive Vice President Scott Guthrie. This restructuring aims to integrate AI-driven security into Microsoft's engineering systems. The move follows security incidents like the 'EchoLeak' flaw in Microsoft 365 Copilot. In March, Microsoft introduced six AI-powered security agents to handle phishing and data monitoring.

Jim Cramer labeled Palantir a '$200 stock masquerading as $144,' citing its new Nuclear Operating System and its importance in government tech procurement. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella recently praised Palantir's Forward Deployed Engineer solution as 'fantastic.' Wedbush analyst Dan Ives suggested Palantir could reach a $1 trillion market capitalization within two to three years.

Microsoft is retiring the 'Blue Screen of Death' after 40 years, replacing it with a black screen for Windows 11 users to provide clearer information on system crashes. The update, which includes a 'Quick Machine Recovery' feature, will roll out later this summer. Microsoft shares reached an all-time high of $469.65 in June, driven by trends in AI and cloud computing.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives raised Microsoft's price target to $600, citing its leadership in cloud computing and hyperscaling. Ives believes Microsoft could reach a $5 trillion valuation within 18 months. Currently, 20% of Microsoft's business is focused on next-gen cloud and AI, which Ives expects to grow to 50-60%. Microsoft's stock is up 18.84% year-to-date, with a consensus price target of $523.73 among 29 analysts.

Amazon subsidiary Ring launched a 'Video Descriptions' feature powered by generative AI for home security. The feature is currently in beta for Ring Home Premium subscribers in the U.S. and Canada. The article notes that in June, Microsoft integrated OpenAI's Sora model into its Bing app to allow users to generate videos from text.

Ant Group invested a record 23.45 billion yuan ($3.26 billion) in R&D during 2024, marking four consecutive years of growth. Parent company Alibaba has committed 380 billion yuan ($52 billion) over three years for AI and cloud infrastructure. Alibaba's strategic AI positioning includes partnerships with Microsoft, Siemens, and Nvidia. Alibaba shares have risen over 58.05% in the past 12 months.

Microsoft stock rose 31% in three months, reaching $500.76 on June 30, 2025. Q3 revenue was $70.07 billion, up 13% Y/Y, with Cloud revenue at $42.4 billion. Azure and cloud services grew 33% Y/Y. Analysts have a consensus price target of $523.73, with some projecting a $4 trillion market cap this summer. Microsoft is renegotiating its revenue share with OpenAI, aiming to reduce it from 20% to 10% by 2030.

Options activity for Microsoft (MSFT) showed a bearish call option sweep expiring on January 16, 2026, with a $400.00 strike price. The trade involved 8 contracts split into 5 trades, with a total cost of $86.2K and a price of $10,786.0 per contract. Open interest at this strike was 3,382 prior to the session, with 1,716 contracts traded.

In the first half of 2025, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose approximately 4%. Microsoft was the fifth-best performer in the index, with an 18.0% gain. Nvidia, a new component added in 2024, gained 17.6%. Apple was the fourth-worst performer, declining 18.1%.

Microsoft implemented a round of layoffs in June 2025, following a reduction of over 6,500 jobs in May. The June cuts impacted roles across engineering, product management, marketing, and legal departments as part of a broader industry trend of tech companies prioritizing AI and operational efficiency.

Microsoft's financial metrics in the software industry show a P/E of 38.33, P/B of 11.45, and P/S of 13.72. The company reports $40.71 billion in EBITDA, $48.15 billion in gross profit, and 13.27% revenue growth. With a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.19, Microsoft demonstrates a stronger financial position than its top four peers. While P/E and P/B ratios suggest the stock may be undervalued, the high P/S ratio indicates potential overvaluation relative to revenue.

Microsoft stock is trading at $497.13, reflecting a 0.24% daily increase, a 7.39% monthly gain, and an 8.24% annual gain. The company's P/E ratio is lower than the software industry's aggregate P/E of 100.8, which may suggest the stock is undervalued or that shareholders have lower growth expectations. The CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Russian SVR chief Sergei Naryshkin have agreed to maintain an open line of communication. This development occurs amid geopolitical tensions, including the Russia-Ukraine conflict. In May 2025, Russian President Vladimir Putin called for restrictions on foreign tech services, specifically naming Microsoft Corporation and Zoom Communications Inc., alleging they undermine Russian sovereignty.

The Arora Report raised its price target for Microsoft to $713–$726, representing a potential 44% gain from its current price of $494.24. Growth is driven by AI monetization, including Copilot integration and Azure's performance. Risks include competition from Amazon's AWS and Google Cloud, potential chip supply constraints, and regulatory scrutiny. Microsoft shares rose 18.48% year-to-date.

OpenAI plans to release an open-source reasoning model next month that reportedly outperforms existing ChatGPT options. Amid industry competition, OpenAI and Microsoft are reportedly in a dispute regarding contractual terms for artificial general intelligence access. OpenAI's annualized revenue run rate reached $10 billion in 2025, with ChatGPT hitting 500 million weekly active users.

Investment manager Dan Niles maintains positions in Microsoft, Nvidia, and Cisco, citing Microsoft's re-accelerating Azure growth. While Niles advises investors to temporarily ignore traditional valuations due to market momentum, he warns of a potential 10-20% correction risk by Thanksgiving due to possible holiday demand disappointment following first-half demand pull-forward.
